draft.title: a cozy coffee shop menu
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a cozy coffee shop menu

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users building cozy coffee shop menus gravitate toward warm, organic, sensory-forward atmospheres over clinical or digital ones
facade.label: Steamed milk and wood
facade.acceptImplies: Menu should emphasize natural textures, handwritten elements, warm lighting, real materials—photos of actual drinks, pressed paper, wood surfaces
facade.rejectImplies: Menu might benefit from cleaner typography, modern photography, minimalist aesthetic, or digital-first presentation

facade.hypothesis: Warm, muted color palette with cream, tan, and deep brown tones creates immediate coziness and coffee shop authenticity
facade.label: Cream and espresso
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward nostalgic, analog color schemes; likely wants vintage or traditional coffee shop aesthetic rather than modern minimalism
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer cooler tones, higher contrast, or more contemporary palettes; wants to avoid looking dated or too brown-heavy

evidence.hypothesis: Users building cozy coffee shop menus gravitate toward warm, organic, sensory-forward atmospheres over clinical or digital ones
evidence.implication: Menu should emphasize natural textures, handwritten elements, warm lighting, real materials—photos of actual drinks, pressed paper, wood surfaces

facade.hypothesis: A cozy coffee shop menu benefits from personal, human-made aesthetics — handwritten fonts, imperfect typography, and warm color palettes that feel curated by the owner rather than mass-produced.
facade.label: Warm and handwritten
facade.acceptImplies: User values personality and authenticity. Menu should lean into owner's voice, hand-drawn elements, imperfect charm, casual tone.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clarity and polish. Menu should use clean typography, consistent formatting, professional visual hierarchy, neutral warmth rather than personality-driven design.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer seeing all items at once in a structured table vs scrolling through narrative descriptions
facade.axisTargeted: Information density — chart/grid vs narrative flow
facade.label: Dense menu grid
facade.acceptImplies: Menu favors comprehensive visual scanning, category-based layout, nutritional/price data visible upfront
facade.rejectImplies: Menu favors storytelling, seasonal highlights, item descriptions, human voice over exhaustive data

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar displaying coffee categories (Espresso, Pour Over, Pastries) with the main content area showing detailed item cards for the selected category.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure: sidebar persistence vs. tab-based switching
facade.label: Sidebar with menu
facade.acceptImplies: The user values consistent, always-visible navigation and structured category browsing—likely prefers desktop-first or hybrid layouts where categories remain accessible while scrolling through items.
facade.rejectImplies: The user may prefer tab-based navigation, modal overlays, or dropdown menus that keep the interface less compartmentalized and more focused on the menu items themselves.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid animated transitions between menu sections over instant jumps
facade.axisTargeted: MOTION: animated transitions vs instant state changes
facade.label: Page slides in smoothly
facade.acceptImplies: Build page flows with easing curves and cross-fades; prioritize motion as part of the cozy experience
facade.rejectImplies: Keep interactions snappy and direct; avoid unnecessary animation that might feel slow or precious

axes.label: Whether user prefers warm, inviting visual aesthetics (earthy tones, soft lighting) vs clean, minimalist design (bright, stark)
Whether user wants items organized by category/type vs browsing as a flowing list or grid
Whether user expects playful, casual tone vs professional, straightforward descriptions of menu items
Whether user seeks cozy, intimate feeling vs energetic, social atmosphere in the space
Whether user wants detailed descriptions (ingredients, origins) vs simple, minimal item names
Whether user prefers smooth, instant interactions vs animations that reveal items gradually

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers warm, inviting visual aesthetics (earthy tones, soft lighting) vs clean, minimalist design (bright, stark)
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants items organized by category/type vs browsing as a flowing list or grid

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants detailed descriptions (ingredients, origins) vs simple, minimal item names
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, instant interactions vs animations that reveal items gradually
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users building cozy coffee shop menus prefer personal, human-made touches over polished uniformity, and will accept slightly imperfect handwritten elements as more authentic and intimate than printed typography
facade.label: Handwritten menu labels
facade.acceptImplies: Menu should feature cursive script, irregular spacing, visible pen strokes, and human imperfections—perhaps menu items labeled by hand or menu boards with chalk or marker lettering
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer consistent, professionally typeset menus with uniform fonts and aligned layouts, suggesting the cozy aesthetic works better through material choices (wood, paper texture) than through handmade typography

draft.title: Cozy Coffee Shop Menu
draft.summary: A warm, inviting digital menu for a coffee shop featuring drinks, pastries, and a welcoming aesthetic with earthy tones and comfortable spacing.
draft.html:
Brew & Crumb

Handcrafted coffee & pastries

Hot Drinks
Espresso $3.50
Rich, bold single or double shot
Cappuccino $5.00
Espresso with steamed milk and foam
Latte $5.50
Smooth espresso with velvety steamed milk
Pastries
Croissant $4.00
Buttery, flaky layers of pastry
Blueberry Muffin $4.50
Fresh blueberries in a tender crumb
